category_definitions.argument-order: Incorrect argument positions in n-ary predicates. Detected by swap_binary_args contrastive probes being entailed (consistent) with the candidate when they should be inconsistent.
category_definitions.quantifier-scope: Wrong quantifier type (universal vs existential), wrong quantifier order, or scope errors. Detected by flip_quantifier contrastive probes being consistent with the candidate.
category_definitions.restrictor-content: Missing, added, or wrong conjuncts in the restrictor/antecedent of a quantified formula. Detected by drop_restrictor_conjunct contrastive probes being consistent with the candidate.
category_definitions.polarity: Negation errors: missing or extra negation on atoms or subformulas. Detected by negate_atom or replace_subformula_with_negation contrastive probes being consistent with the candidate.
category_definitions.connective-polarity: Wrong logical connective: and/or confusion, implication/biconditional confusion, or reversed implication direction. Detected by flip_connective contrastive probes being consistent with the candidate.
category_definitions.content-gap: Missing semantic content: the candidate fails to entail one or more sub-entailment positive probes derived from the gold. Indicates omitted predicates, missing conjuncts, or structural incompleteness.

mapping_rationale.negate_atom_to_polarity: Negating an atom is a direct polarity error. When the candidate has wrong polarity, the negate_atom contrastive (which IS correctly negated relative to gold) will be consistent with the candidate instead of inconsistent.
mapping_rationale.swap_binary_args_to_argument_order: Direct correspondence: swapping arguments in a predicate IS the argument-order error.
mapping_rationale.flip_quantifier_to_quantifier_scope: Flipping quantifier type (forall<->exists) is the canonical quantifier-scope/type error.
mapping_rationale.drop_restrictor_conjunct_to_restrictor_c: Dropping a restrictor conjunct directly tests whether the candidate preserves restrictor content.
mapping_rationale.flip_connective_to_connective_polarity: Connective flips (and<->or, implies<->iff, direction reversal) are a family of polarity-like errors at the connective level, distinct from atom-level polarity.
mapping_rationale.replace_subformula_with_negation_to_pola: Wrapping a subformula in negation is structurally a polarity error at a larger scope.
mapping_rationale.positive_failure_to_content_gap: When a positive sub-entailment probe fails, the candidate is missing content that the gold formula entails. This is a structural completeness issue.

note: All 6 contrastive operators plus the positive-failure family map to one of 6 categories with no residual. 7/7 families mapped = 100%.
